title: Valara Saar
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Valara|more=Saar|more2=Prophetess}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Valara.jpg]] |name=Valara Saar |homeworld=[[Yashuvhu/Legends|Yashuvhu]]{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=sw20010517b_valara&c=rpg|text=Meet Valara Saar: Force Adept}} |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] ([[Yashuvhi]]) |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=Dark |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] (temporarily) *[[Yashuvhi]] |masters= |apprentices=Unidentified Yashuvhi citizens }} {{Dialogue|Saba|How long haz ''[sic]'' she been there?|Mara|The records don't say, but if she was trained as a child, it could be decades.|Jacen|Either that or she found a Holocron.|Luke|Let's not jump to any hasty conclusions. Strictly speaking, this isn't what we're here to look for.|attr=The Jedi [[Saba Sebatyne]], [[Mara Jade Skywalker]], [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] discuss the discovery of Valara Saar in Chiss records while searching for Zonama Sekot|src=''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic II: Refugee]]''}} '''Valara Saar''', also known as the '''Prophetess''', was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Force Adept]] from [[Yashuvhu/Legends|Yashuvhu]] who lived sometime during the waning years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and into the reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. When the Republic vessel ''[[Pathfinder III]]'' landed on Yashuvhu, Saar eagerly learned of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and sought to join the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. Being skilled with the learning of [[Language/Legends|language]], she quickly acquired a facility with [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]]. As a result, she mediated between the native [[Yashuvhi]] and the Republic crew, and eventually convinced the first contact team to take her off-world to become a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. In spite of her skill and eagerness to learn, she was unable to join the Order and proceeded to wander. After a period aimlessly traveling [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], she returned to her native [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. There, living isolated in the [[Yashaka Mountains]], she continued learning of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], and became a teacher to some of the Yashuvhi citizens. Her teachings, along with her encounter with the [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]], were rediscovered in [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]] by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] on his search for [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]]. Saar also had two [[duuvhal]]s, which she kept as pets, but were also her companions and trained to fight. The first duuvhal, [[Zeev]], was adopted at a young age but died in a skirmish on [[Aargau/Legends|Aargau]] during her time of wandering. She later adopted another duuvhal, which she named [[Jedi (duuvhal)|"Jedi"]] when she resettled on Yashuvhu. ==Biography== Saar was a descendant of a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] who had crashed on the incommunicado [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Yashuvhu/Legends|Yashuvhu]] sometime during the time of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]]. Saar's talents with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] were evident when she was a small child; she also inherited the [[Yashuvhi sacred scroll|sacred scroll]], a weathered document on which her long-dead relative had written a part of the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code]]. In addition, Saar adopted a [[duuvhal]] as a pet, early in her life, naming it "[[Zeev]]."{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=sw20010517d_duuvhal&c=rpg|text=Meet Zeev, a Pet Duuvhal}} In the years leading up to the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20020708crpg|text=''Star Wars Roleplaying Game'' Overview}} the Republic exploratory [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] ''[[Pathfinder III]]'' landed on Yashuvhu in the course of its first-contact mission. They discovered that the Human population wanted to talk with the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] among the vessel's crew and learn about them. The first-contact specialists were able to teach the [[Yashuvhi]] some information about the Jedi, which was accepted with religious fervor. It was Valara Saar, however, who was the most interested of the Yashuvhi. Her ability to quickly learn and understand language enabled her to communicate with the explorers, who spoke [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] instead of the [[Yashuvhi language|native language]]. [[File:Saar.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Valara Saar alongside [[Arani Korden]] and [[Vor'en Kurn]]]] Saar convinced the ''Pathfinder III'' crew to take her to other worlds, with the hope of following the path of the Jedi. Nevertheless, the Jedi were too restrictive for her free-spirited soul. While she was taught some important skills and worked diligently, she eventually left the Jedi without any hope of joining the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Order]]. Disillusioned, Saar decided instead to look for her destiny elsewhere in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], becoming a wanderer. She exchanged her Force skills for passage to other planets. She helped those in need wherever she found them, and continued to learn more and more of the Force without the guidance of a master as she bartered her skills for passage from [[Planet/Legends|planet]] to planet. Saar also made a number of important friends during her travels. During one such mission on [[Aargau/Legends|Aargau]], however, her pet and companion [[duuvhal]], Zeev, was killed. After years of wandering, Saar eventually returned to her native Yashuvhu, even though her friends had promised to take her anywhere she would need or want to go. She isolated herself, living in a hut ensconced somewhere in the [[Yashaka Mountains]]. The Yashuvhi began referring to her as the "Prophetess" and protector of the people. She became a teacher of the Force, never teaching more than what she felt her students were ready to learn, and learning much at the same time. During this period, Saar developed her own philosophical tenets, which she taught to her students. Her teachings were recognizably derived from the Jedi Code and extended philosophies. She also took a new duuvhal pup to replace Zeev, calling it [[Jedi (duuvhal)|"Jedi."]] This pup was her only companion, as she never had a mate. Although Saar lived in isolation, she was known as a defender of her people, particularly against the [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]]. In [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]], [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] discovered a series of documents in the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] [[Expeditionary Library]] on [[Csilla/Legends|Csilla]] about Valara Saar and her home planet while searching for information regarding [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]]. Contained in the [[Cheunh/Legends|Cheunh]] text was a mention of a Chiss landing party that had "only recently" arrived on Yashuvhu. Saar—who had become advanced in years, according to the records—initially did not meet with the Chiss, even though some of her followers had. When the team approached her dwelling, however, she forcibly expelled them from the planet. The Chiss records strongly indicated that she wielded what appeared to be a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] during the encounter. ==Personality and traits== Valara Saar was a free-spirited [[Force Adept]], who was already accomplished as a Force-user by the time the ''Pathfinder III'' crew arrived on Yashuvhu. She was an eager learner of the ways of the Jedi, having been exposed to portions of the Jedi Code through the sacred scroll that had been passed down from earlier generations. After convincing the first-contact crew to take her off-world, she attempted to join the Jedi Order. After learning a few basic skills, however, she was not accepted—due in part to her free-spirited nature. As a result, Saar became somewhat disillusioned and believed that she could only grow in the Force when traveling the galaxy. After wandering for a time, she eventually resettled on Yashuvhu, but did consider traveling again with the assistance of those she met earlier in life. Saar was also intelligent, being able to quickly learn new languages, as demonstrated by her ability to quickly learn Basic when encountering the crew of the ''Pathfinder III''. She continuously sought knowledge, learning of the Force even without a master to train her. She also raised at least two duuvhals, which she trained for combat and were her constant companions. Saar was considered a judicious instructor, only teaching her followers what they were prepared to learn. Even while teaching, she herself continued to grow in knowledge of the Force. Saar's teachings included many aspects derived from the Jedi Code and philosophical practices. These included patience, humility, and compassion, along with clarity of thought, balance between physical and mental prowess, strict observance of diet, and a solitary lifestyle. In leading the lifestyle of her teachings, she never took a mate or had any children. ==Powers and abilities== {{Quote|Look at this.''"
"''She's talking about the Force.''"
"''I think so.|Luke and Mara Jade Skywalker reading a description of Saar in the Expeditionary Library on Csilla|}} [[File:ValaraSaarHumanForceAdept.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Valara Saar, Human Force adept]] When Valara Saar first came in contact with the Republic representatives aboard the ''Pathfinder III'', she was already a skilled Force user, though untrained and considered a wild talent. She was able to learn some skills from the Jedi, before wandering the galaxy. During this time, she used her skills to barter for passage for herself to travel. Upon returning to Yashuvhu, she became a teacher in the Force and was revered for her ability in combat. Nobody could visit her if she was not willing, as her fighting skill was greater than any other, even apart from her Force abilities. She continued to grow in the Force and may have developed the ability to create [[Mind trick/Legends|Force Illusions]].{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030403counseling|series=Jedi Counseling|text=11: Illusion Confusion}} Her ability to fight and use the Force was displayed when she drove a landing party of Chiss from the planet. The skirmish was well-documented in the Expeditionary Library. ==Equipment== {{Quote|She's wielding a lightsaber!''"
"''It looks very much like it.|Jacen Solo and Luke Skywalker, discovering mention of Saar in Chiss records|}} Valara Saar, during the era of the ''Pathfinder III'' encounter, was known to have used a [[Quarterstaff/Legends|quarterstaff]] and sling in combat. She was known to have been accompanied by Zeev, a pet duuvhal whom she had raised from a pup. The animal accompanied her and could bite someone upon her command, inflicting them with a poisonous venom. Zeev served Saar until his death in a skirmish on [[Aargau/Legends|Aargau]]. Upon her return to Yashuvhu, she adopted and trained another duuvhal pup, which she named "Jedi." She was also known to have skill with a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and continued to use her quarterstaff. During the encounter with a team from the Chiss Ascendancy, it was documented that she wielded what appeared to have been a lightsaber. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Force_Adept_SWM.jpg|thumb|left|116px|Human Force Adept miniature, based upon Valara Saar]] No definitive date has been established for the lifetime of Valara Saar. While the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'', Saar's first appearance, covers the eras of the six ''[[Star Wars]]'' films, no date can be derived any more precisely than the era in which the events of her life took place. Discerning an exact date is further compounded by the text of ''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic II: Refugee]]'', published the year following the web enhancement. In 28 ABY, the planet Yashuvhu is described as encountering the Chiss "only recently," while the following sentence states that Valara Saar was an "ancient woman called the Prophetess who oversaw the spiritual development of the colony." On the other hand, the novel states that she expelled the Chiss landing party when they attempted to approach her dwelling. The initial reference to the spiritual development of the colony might be analogous to Saar's original appearance in the role-playing supplement ''Meet Valara Saar: Force Adept'', which states that an unidentified Jedi became marooned on the planet Yashuvhu "many thousands of years" prior to the lifetime of Valara Saar. This Jedi began a Force-sensitive bloodline and left a parchment containing part of the Jedi Code. Valara Saar's image was first used in the ''Star Wars Roleplaying Game'' as an example of a first-level character from the [[Force Adept]] class. Her background would be later provided by [[Wizards of the Coast]] on its website.''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Core Rulebook]]'' The background information was written by [[Cory J. Herndon]]. Her likeness is also used in [[Alliance and Empire (Star Wars Miniatures)|''Star Wars Miniatures: Alliance and Empire'']] to represent a "generic Human Force Adept."{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/AEPreview3|text=''Alliance and Empire'' Preview 3}} While not definitively [[canon]], Valara Saar also has a brief appearance in a web enhancement and help supplement to the ''Star Wars Roleplaying Game'', titled ''Jedi Counseling 11: Illusion Confusion''. In this supplement, Saar is used in an example whereby she casts a [[Mind trick/Legends|Force illusion]] of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] to frighten some guards. Also mentioned in this supplement is an encounter with at least one [[Yuuzhan Vong]]. ==Appearances== *''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic II: Refugee]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Valara Saar}} *''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Core Rulebook]]'' {{1stm}} *''[[Living Force Campaign Guide]]'' {{Po}} *{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=sw20010517b_valara&c=rpg|text=Meet Valara Saar: Force Adept}} *{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=sw20010517d_duuvhal&c=rpg|text=Meet Zeev, a Pet Duuvhal}} *{{GamerCite|10|A Revised Rulebook Companion}} {{Po}} *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030403counseling|series=Jedi Counseling|text=11: Illusion Confusion}} *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/AEPreview3|text=''Alliance and Empire'' Preview 3}} *''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook]]'' {{Po}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Valara Saar }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saar, Valara}} [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Force Adepts]] [[Category:Humans]]
